title: elymus lazicus subsp. lazicus
intro: Elymus lazicus subsp. lazicus, also known as the Eastern Elymus or Turkish wheat grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of the Middle East and Eastern Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in folk medicine, for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Elymus lazicus subsp. lazicus include reducing inflammation and pain, treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and aiding in digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Elymus lazicus subsp. lazicus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
